rationales.self_direction: He emphasizes doing it "exactly how I wanted it to" and celebrates "Eight years of building this thing on my own terms," showing strong commitment to choosing his own path.
rationales.achievement: Closing the deal and keeping the contract clean reflects clear success against a demanding professional standard.
rationales.power: He notes "They came around because they knew I'd walk and they needed me more than I needed them," highlighting leverage and control in the negotiation.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Derek's advice and insists, "I just don't want someone else's version of success stapled to my forehead," defending an independently chosen life.
rationales.conformity: He openly resists family expectations about how he should "scale properly" and what success should look like, rather than smoothing over the disagreement.

rationales.self_direction: Walking away from "three years of dealing with their approval committees" and saying "Not going back to that" shows a strong preference for working on his own terms.
rationales.security: He gives up a contract where "The money was steady" because stability matters less to him than freedom from bureaucracy.
rationales.conformity: He rejects the approval-heavy client structure and dismisses the expectation to stay within that bureaucratic process.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes because they offered "full creative control over the architecture" and because the project has "no corporate layers," showing clear preference for independent work.
rationales.achievement: Being treated "like an expert, not a vendor" and hired for a specialized build reflects recognition of competence.
rationales.power: The clients "trusted me to build it" and deferred to his judgment, giving him unusual influence over the work.

rationales.self_direction: He chooses to keep "figuring things out myself even when it's slower," favoring independent problem-solving over relying on others.
rationales.achievement: He links solving it alone with having "really earn[ed] the solution," showing a strong need to prove competence through his own effort.

rationales.self_direction: He celebrates that "I control the timeline" and "I decide when to push hard and when to step back," making personal control central to the day.
rationales.hedonism: He takes a "long walk just to clear my head," orders Thai food, and works late "because I wanted to," framing pleasure and chosen enjoyment as important.
rationales.achievement: He notes "Finished the database schema and got their sign-off on the first prototype wireframes," highlighting concrete progress and successful performance.

rationales.hedonism: He has "Haven't slept more than four hours in three days" and plans to work "through the holidays," sacrificing wellbeing and enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He refuses to reset expectations because "I quoted the timeline. I own it," showing strong commitment to meeting a difficult standard.
rationales.benevolence: The pressure spills onto close relationships: he is "snapping at the barista" and tells Rachel he'll try for Christmas when "We both knew I was lying."
He calls Rachel to apologize for Christmas and says he may need to change how he works, showing more willingness to care for a close relationship instead of only the project.

rationales.achievement: He stays late to keep "running another simulation batch," prioritizing dissertation progress and future advancement.
rationales.benevolence: He misses Layla's first swimming lesson and admits, "I didn't try," putting work ahead of an important family moment.
He admits he "lean[s] on her for everything outside the lab" and that it has stopped feeling like partnership, showing neglect of a close relationship.
He tells Dina "she absolutely should" take the class and commits to handling the kids, explicitly making space for something that is important to her.

rationales.hedonism: He pulls "two all-nighters" and says his "Body is wrecked," clearly sacrificing rest and wellbeing.
rationales.achievement: Submitting the chapter at "4am" after an intense push shows strong effort to meet an academic performance target.
rationales.benevolence: He "snapped at her" and recognizes he has been "neglecting everything that wasn't the dissertation," harming someone close to him.
He gives Dina a full apology, acknowledges he was unfair, and chooses to be present for a "Normal family morning" instead of returning to work immediately.

rationales.achievement: He notes the advisor "approved the revised chapter with minor edits," a clear marker of competence and progress.
rationales.benevolence: He says "This arrangement is working and I want to protect it," showing ongoing effort to preserve time that supports Dina and the kids.

rationales.achievement: He fixates on being "behind" in "Conference papers, publications" and "The CV metrics that actually matter for the job market," showing strong concern with measurable success.

rationales.benevolence: He feels he "failed them" because his parents could not "lean on me," and he immediately considers visiting, showing strong concern for close family despite being overwhelmed.
